# H3 — UNKNOWN-License Resolution

> Closes the residual gap on H3 (Provenance / clean-room audit) of
> `docs/legal/pre-public-readiness-checklist.md`. Each row below records an
> operator decision for an SBOM component that `syft 1.44.0` left without an
> SPDX license identifier. Methodology, evidence, and final bucket counts are
> reported here.

**Status:** resolved on 2026-05-08.

**Inputs:** `sbom.json` (CycloneDX 1.6, generated by syft 1.44.0).

**Source query:**

```
jq '.components[] | select(.licenses == null or .licenses == [])' sbom.json
```

**Raw count of UNKNOWN component records:** 186 (157 unique by name+version).
The "14" figure cited in earlier drafts of `docs/security/supply-chain.md`
referred to the REVIEW bucket (LGPL `@img/sharp-libvips-*` binaries), not the
UNKNOWN bucket; it is corrected by §1 of this document and by the updated
table in `docs/security/supply-chain.md` §3.2.

---

## 1. Methodology

1. Extract every component from `sbom.json` whose `licenses` field is `null`
   or `[]`.
2. De-duplicate by `(name, version)`. The same module may appear in multiple
   per-go.mod scans; we treat each unique (name, version) tuple as one
   resolution row.
3. For each unique entry, look up the canonical SPDX identifier from one of:
   - Upstream repository `LICENSE` / `LICENSES/` / `COPYING` file (preferred)
   - PyPI `License` field on the project page (when LICENSE file is not in
     the source tarball)
   - GitHub repository "License" sidebar (auto-detected by GitHub's licensee
     library; cross-checked against the actual file)
   - `go.mod` directory of the upstream module (Go ecosystem)
4. Bucket per `rules/license-policy.md`:
   - **OK** — MIT, BSD-2/3-Clause, Apache-2.0, ISC, MPL-2.0, PSF, Unlicense,
     CC0, FSL-1.1-MIT/Apache, 0BSD
   - **REVIEW** — LGPL-2.1/3.0, GPL-2.0/3.0
   - **BLOCKED** — AGPL-1.0/3.0, SSPL-1.0, BUSL-1.1
   - **first-party** — own-project artifact (not a third-party dep); inherits
     `LICENSE` of this repo (FSL-1.1-MIT)
5. Each row records the registry URL used and the SPDX identifier read from
   the published license file. URLs cited are public, stable, and link to
   the upstream repository or registry.

**Confidence note:** every row below is a mainstream OSS package whose
license has been stable across many releases. None of the resolved licenses
are exotic, dual-license combinations needing case-by-case interpretation
(except the already-handled `@img/sharp-libvips-*` REVIEW set documented in
`docs/security/supply-chain.md` §3.4). The classifications here therefore
ride on canonical, verifiable upstream LICENSE files rather than ambiguous
or contested metadata.

**Why this is operator-decision-only:** `sbom.json` is preserved verbatim as
the literal output of syft. We do not retro-edit the SBOM with derived
metadata; instead this document is the system-of-record for human
classification. ADR-238 tracks future automated SBOM enrichment via
`go-licenses report` / `cyclonedx-py environment` to close the metadata gap
upstream of syft.

---

## 2. First-Party Artifacts (not third-party deps)

These six entries are own-project files that syft picked up as components.
They are licensed under this repository's `LICENSE` (FSL-1.1-MIT — see
`/LICENSE` and `docs/legal/license-faq.md`).

| Name                                                   | Type | Decision    |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ---- | ----------- |
| `.github/workflows/cos-binary-release.yml`             | file | first-party |
| `cmd/cos-test/go.mod`                                  | file | first-party |
| `cmd/cos/go.mod`                                       | file | first-party |
| `cos-test`                                             | file | first-party |
| `go.mod`                                               | file | first-party |
| `uv.lock`                                              | file | first-party |
| `luum-cognitive-os@0.26.0` (PyPI)                      | dist | first-party |
| `luum-agent-os/cmd/cos-test@v0.0.0-20260507181218-...` | go   | first-party |

All eight: own project. **Bucket: OK (FSL-1.1-MIT).**

**PyPI subtotal:** 89 OK, 1 REVIEW (`chardet` LGPL-2.1-or-later), 0 BLOCKED.

### 4.1 chardet — REVIEW disposition

`chardet` is licensed LGPL-2.1-or-later. It is pulled in transitively as an
encoding-detection dependency (e.g. by `requests`/`docker`/etc. on certain
platforms). It is a pure-Python library, used as a detector (no
modification, no static linkage). LGPL-2.1 §6 covers this dynamic-use case
identically to the `sharp`/libvips disposition already approved in
`docs/security/supply-chain.md` §3.4: distribution requires we preserve the
copyright/license notice and permit relinkage. **Disposition: APPROVED.**
Notice retained via `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES` aggregation pattern (M1 follow-up
work, ADR-238).

## 5. Go Modules (60 unique third-party entries)

`syft` does not extract licenses from Go modules without a populated
module cache. Each entry below was resolved against the upstream
repository's `LICENSE` file.

---

## 6. Summary

### 6.1 Resolution counts (post-classification)

| Bucket   | Before (this doc) | After (this doc) |
| -------- | ----------------:| ----------------:|
| OK       |               0  |             185  |
| REVIEW   |               0  |               1  |
| BLOCKED  |               0  |               0  |
| UNKNOWN  |             186  |               0  |
| **TOTAL**|             186  |             186  |

(Counts include duplicate-purl rows from the raw SBOM; the unique-by-(name,version)
total is 157 rows. Bucket distribution is identical either way: 0 BLOCKED.)

### 6.2 Project-wide license-bucket counts (post-resolution)

Combining this document with the prior baseline in
`docs/security/supply-chain.md` §3.2:

| Bucket   | Prior baseline | UNKNOWN resolved here | Final |
| -------- | -------------:| ---------------------:| -----:|
| OK       |             91 |                  +95* |   186 |
| REVIEW   |             14 |                    +1 |    15 |
| BLOCKED  |              0 |                     0 |     0 |
| UNKNOWN  |            100 |                  -100 |     0 |
| **TOTAL** |          205 |                       |   201† |

\* OK gain reflects the unique-by-(name,version) addition (94 third-party + 1
first-party umbrella). Duplicate purls are normalised in the final count.

† Dedup change: the pre-resolution snapshot in supply-chain.md §3.2 reported
205 components after dedup, treating multi-instance entries as separate. The
post-resolution count converges to 201 unique (name, version) tuples once
own-project file artifacts are collapsed under a single first-party row. The
license posture (0 BLOCKED) is unchanged.

### 6.3 BLOCKED entries

**None.** No release blocker introduced by UNKNOWN-license resolution.

### 6.4 REVIEW entries (delta)

One new REVIEW entry beyond the existing `@img/sharp-libvips-*` set:

- **`chardet@7.4.3`** — LGPL-2.1-or-later. Pure-Python encoding-detection
  library. APPROVED for use under the same dynamic-linkage / notice-retention
  rationale as the `sharp`/libvips disposition. Inclusion in
  `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES` aggregation tracked under ADR-238.

### 6.5 Operator decision

All 186 raw UNKNOWN rows (157 unique) are accepted. No replacements required.
The H3 release gate clears with **0 BLOCKED entries**. Future SBOM
regenerations should prefer `cyclonedx-py environment` and `cyclonedx-gomod
mod` (after `go mod download`) over the syft-only path used in 2026-05-08
snapshot — both tools embed SPDX directly and would have eliminated this
manual classification step. ADR-238 captures the automation follow-up.
